prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Here are four sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler documenting SaaS Q3 results:

---

"In the third quarter of this year of Our Lord, the wizards of the Cloud did conjure forth a Monthly Recurring Revenue of two million ducats, though I confess I searched the sky most diligently and found no cloud from which such coin might fall."

"The churn rate, which I had assumed to be a device for the making of butter, did instead consume fourteen percent of the customer serfs, who vanished from the kingdom without siege or pestilence to explain their departure."

"It is recorded that the Sales team did 'crush their pipeline,' and though I dispatched a monk to inspect the pipes beneath the castle, he returned unharmed and deeply puzzled, reporting only carpet."

"The Board of Directors, a council of lords who convene by magic mirror from distant lands, expressed cautious optimism about the roadmap, which I have examined and which leads, as best I can determine, nowhere, being composed entirely of words and containing no roads whatsoever."
